text: Reminder: All-staff meeting is Thursday Oct 22 at 10:00 AM in the main conference room (3300 N Federal Hwy). Agenda will be circulated by Tuesday EOD. Please confirm attendance by replying here.
Confirmed — I'll be there.
Confirmed. I have a 9 AM appointment that ends by 9:45 so I'll head straight over.
Confirmed. I'll have the conference room set up by 9:45.
Scheduling reminder: Dr. Foster has requested coverage Nov 6–7 (conference). Please review your availability and respond in this channel by Oct 20.
I can cover morning slots on Nov 6. Rachel, can you share your usual schedule so I can see where gaps are?

Heads up: we have 4 outstanding insurance pre-auth requests that are 10+ business days old. I'll follow up by phone today. Karen, one of them may require a letter of medical necessity — I'll flag it if so.
Thanks Danielle. If you need a letter of medical necessity drafted, drop me a note here and I'll turn it around same day.

The new EHR intake template goes live November 1. Rachel will lead a 30-minute walkthrough for all clinical staff — we'll set the time at the Oct 22 meeting. Please flag any questions about the new template format in this channel.
Good morning, Bell Behavioral Health team. Office opens in 30 minutes. Today is Friday Oct 16 — have a great day.
Supervision sessions for this week: Darius and Monique on Tuesday, Tanya and Grace on Wednesday, Eduardo on Thursday. Please bring one case for formulation discussion using the CFI framework.
Thanks Dr. Bell — I'll bring an intake from my Tuesday medication-management shadowing with Dr. Foster.
Same here. I have a question about screening for intergenerational trauma — saving it for our session.

NAMI Florida panel deck is about 60% done. Still need the case vignette for session 3. Driving down to Miami Beach the night before — booked Three for dinner. FNA keynote outline is in Google Drive if anyone wants to review.
Happy to review the FNA outline. Strongly suggest opening with the Broward access-gap data — it landed really well at the Coalition.
Posted the v2 hero with the warm-sand palette and the sticky 'Book Speaking' CTA. Mobile mockups are in Figma — link in the canvas. Let us know if the bio section spacing works for you.
Love it. Warm-sand over teal is the right call. One note — please make the credentials badge (PMHNP-BC) a bit more prominent in the hero. Otherwise approved to move forward.
October newsletter is ready to schedule — 'Managing seasonal stress' plus the NAMI Broward workshop recap. Karen, want to review before it goes out Friday?
Yes, send me the draft. Let's add a line linking to the Cultural Competence CME workbook on the Bell Clinical Press store.
Mom, the storefront page links all three guides to checkout now. Want me to add the CME workbook once the cover is final?
Yes please, Oliver — Coral Ridge is finalizing the CME workbook cover this week. Thank you for setting up checkout!
